description: Assign a driver to an order and dispatch the order to that driver, supporting both manual assignment and proximity-based adhoc dispatch.

actors.name: Dispatcher
actors.description: Operator or system that assigns drivers to orders.

actors.name: Driver
actors.description: The driver receiving the dispatched order.

fields.name: order_id
fields.label: Identifier of the order to dispatch.

fields.name: driver_id
fields.label: Public identifier of the driver to assign (omit for adhoc mode).

fields.name: adhoc
fields.label: If true, order is broadcast to nearby drivers; first to accept is assigned.

fields.name: adhoc_distance
fields.label: Radius in meters within which nearby drivers are pinged for adhoc orders.

fields.name: dispatch_immediately
fields.label: Whether to dispatch the order immediately after assignment.

fields.name: dispatched
fields.label: Whether the order has been successfully dispatched.

rules.rule_01: A driver can only be assigned to one active order at a time (current_job).
rules.rule_02: Assigning a driver does not automatically dispatch; dispatch is a separate explicit action.
rules.rule_03: In adhoc mode, the order is broadcast to all available drivers within adhoc_distance; the first driver to accept is assigned.
rules.rule_04: A dispatched order cannot be dispatched a second time.
rules.rule_05: If no driver is assigned and adhoc is false, dispatch fails with ORDER_NO_DRIVER_ASSIGNED.
rules.rule_06: When a driver is assigned, the driver receives a push notification with order details.
rules.rule_07: Vehicle assignment is exclusive; a vehicle can only be active for one driver at a time.

given: driver is available (not on another active order)

then.description: Order record is updated with the assigned driver reference.
Driver receives push notification informing them of the new order assignment.

driver_manually_assigned.result: Driver is linked to the order and notified. Order is ready to dispatch.

then.description: All available drivers within adhoc_distance are pinged with the order details.

adhoc_ping_sent.result: Nearby drivers receive a ping and can accept or reject the order.

given: driver is assigned OR adhoc is true
order has not been dispatched already
order_dispatched.result: Order is live; driver begins navigating to pickup.

given: a driver accepts the order ping
no other driver has already accepted

then.description: First-responding driver is locked to the order.
Order is set as the driver's current active job.

driver_assigned_at_adhoc_accept.result: Driver is assigned; order proceeds with this driver.

dispatch_failed_no_driver.result: Dispatch is rejected; operator must assign a driver first.

errors.message: This order has already been dispatched.
The specified driver could not be found.

events.name: order.driver_assigned
events.description: Fired when a driver is linked to an order.

events.name: order.dispatched
events.description: Fired when an order is dispatched (to a specific driver or broadcast in adhoc mode).

events.name: order.dispatch_failed
events.description: Fired when dispatch cannot be completed.

related.reason: Dispatch is a key state transition in the ride lifecycle.
Driver app handles the ping, accept, and reject interaction.
Only online/on-shift drivers should receive pings.
Vehicle assignment is validated as part of driver dispatch readiness.

goals.description: Assign a driver to an order and dispatch the order to that driver, supporting both manual assignment and proximity-based adhoc dispatch.

success_metrics.measurement: Successful operations divided by total attempts

tradeoffs.reason: workflow steps must complete correctly before proceeding
